fresh_facts.claim: President Trump announced on April 11, 2026 that US forces will remain in Iran until Tehran agrees to a new nuclear and regional deal.
US Central Command has conducted over 7,000 targets hit in Iran, with ballistic missile attacks down 90% as of April 11, 2026.
US intelligence assesses Iran retains over 1,000 medium-range ballistic missiles as of April 11, 2026.
US and Israeli Defense Forces are jointly conducting Operation Epic Fury with reported successful strikes over nine days as of April 11, 2026.
Iran's Supreme National Security Council accepted a Pakistan-mediated two-week ceasefire as of April 11, 2026.
High-stakes US-Iran diplomatic talks began in Islamabad on April 10-11, 2026, with Iran's delegation including Ghalibaf and Araghchi.
Iran's delegation demanded Lebanon truce and frozen asset release as preconditions for US nuclear deal negotiations as of April 11, 2026.
The Strait of Hormuz remains closed despite the ceasefire, spiking global oil prices as of April 11, 2026.
President Pezeshkian faces backlash from hardliners and the IRGC for conditional willingness to end the war as of April 11, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei has risen as the new supreme leader post-Khamenei, issuing defiant compensation demands as of April 11, 2026.
A Provisional Leadership Council consisting of Pezeshkian, Mohseni-Ejei, and Arafi holds interim power following a leadership crisis as of April 11, 2026.
Hardliners have labeled ex-Foreign Minister Zarif a traitor warranting arrest as of April 11, 2026.
The assassination of Ali Larijani has exposed security gaps within Iran's regime as of April 11, 2026.
Iran's economy shows structural crisis with currency distrust, high inflation, unemployment, and war-driven oil disruptions as of April 11, 2026.
Prediction markets show 8.5% odds of Iranian regime fall by June 30, 2026, down from 12% previously.
The UN Noon Briefing on April 10, 2026 condemned Lebanese strikes linked to Iran tensions for casualties and displacement.
Trump threatened additional military strikes against Iran if diplomatic talks in Islamabad fail as of April 11, 2026.
US Vice President Vance, along with envoys Wickoff and Kushner, traveled to Pakistan for diplomatic talks as of April 10-11, 2026.
Iran may call off Islamabad talks scheduled for the weekend of April 11-12, rejecting US terms as unreasonable as of April 11, 2026.
A-10 Warthogs and AH-64 helicopters are conducting operations on Iran's southern flank and against Iranian-aligned militias in Iraq as of April 11, 2026.
Iran's ballistic missile arsenal has decreased from pre-war levels of approximately 2,500 to over 1,000 as of April 11, 2026.
Assembly of Experts strike has delayed succession proceedings in Iran as of April 11, 2026.
State media has warned Iranian officials against revealing their locations amid ongoing turmoil as of April 11, 2026.
Social unrest in Iran is rising with a weakened military, though state media frames protests as enemy warfare as of April 11, 2026.
CSIS noted that the fragile ceasefire hides unresolved nuclear risks and terrorism threats as of April 11, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Following devastating US military strikes in early April 2026 that degraded Iran's military capabilities by 80-95% and a collapsed ceasefire, Iran faces critical pressures across military, economic, diplomatic, and internal stability fronts. The regime is attempting to consolidate control through internal security crackdowns while managing deep institutional fractures, succession disputes, and widespread economic crisis.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Post-ceasefire repression surge including over 700 arrests, IRGC/Basij raids across Iranian cities, and fatal shootings in Khomein and Hamedan on July 2, 2026, indicating regime consolidating control through internal security operations amid military losses.
